title: Daucalis leptophylla
taxonomy: Daucalis leptophylla is classified within the family Rutaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. This family consists of over 160 genera and more than 1,500 species. Within the Rutaceae family, Daucalis leptophylla is further classified as a member of the subfamily Rutoideae. The exact classification of Daucalis leptophylla is as follows: family Rutaceae, subfamily Rutoideae, genus Daucalis, species D. leptophylla. Further classification may include tribe and section levels, but these are not specified in this context.
uses: Daucalis leptophylla, a plant species found in various regions, is reported to have medicinal uses. intro: Daucalis leptophylla, also known as Puerto Rican buttonwood or simply buttonwood, is a flowering plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the island of Puerto Rico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health issues such as fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Daucalis leptophylla include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, and it is also sometimes used in aromatherapy and as a natural remedy for respiratory issues.
